manufacturer.comment: LattePanda is a brand operated by DFRobot.

model.comment: The 864 suffix denotes 8GB RAM + 64GB eMMC; this is the only retail configuration.

release-date.comment: Globally available August 2022 via DFRobot after a Kickstarter campaign launched in November 2021.

status.comment: Still listed and sold by DFRobot and the official LattePanda site as of 2026-04-26.

open-source-hardware.comment: No PCB design files released under an OSHW-compatible license; only block diagrams and pinouts in docs.

country-of-origin.comment: DFRobot is headquartered in Shanghai, China.

max-clock-speed-ghz.comment: Base 2.0 GHz, burst up to 2.9 GHz per official spec.

process-node.comment: Jasper Lake is fabricated on Intel 10 nm (now "Intel 7" naming was applied to later nodes; Jasper Lake remains 10 nm SuperFin).

opengl-es-version.comment: Gen11 supports OpenGL ES 3.2 / OpenGL 4.6 on Linux Mesa drivers.

vulkan-support.comment: Gen11 supports Vulkan 1.3 via Mesa ANV / Intel Windows drivers.

npu.comment: No dedicated NPU; AI workloads use the iGPU or CPU.

hw-video-decode.comment: Jasper Lake/Gen11 supports HW decode for H.264, HEVC (8/10-bit), and VP9. AV1 is NOT hardware-decoded on Gen11; AV1 acceleration first appears in Gen12 (Tiger Lake / Alder Lake-N).

hw-video-encode.comment: Quick Sync on Gen11 covers H.264, HEVC, and VP9 encode.

ram-min-gb.comment: Only 8 GB SKU is sold; no smaller variant.

ram-max-gb.comment: RAM is soldered LPDDR4 and not user-upgradable.

microsd-slot.comment: Micro-SD + Micro-SIM combo slot adjacent to the M.2 M-key socket.

m2-nvme-slot.comment: M.2 M-key 2280 supports PCIe 3.0 x2 NVMe SSDs. A separate M.2 B-key slot supports SATA III SSDs and 4G/5G modems.

sata.comment: Native SATA III is exposed via the M.2 B-key slot, not a dedicated SATA connector.

ethernet.comment: Current units ship with Intel I225-V at 2.5 GbE. Pre-June 2023 batches used Intel I211-AT at 1 GbE.

dual-ethernet.comment: Single onboard RJ-45; a second NIC requires an M.2 add-in card.

bluetooth.comment: Bluetooth 5.2 via the Intel AX201 module.

usb-a-3x-ports.comment: 1× USB 3.2 Gen 2 (10 Gbps) + 2× USB 3.2 Gen 1 (5 Gbps) Type-A.

usb-a-2-ports.comment: No external USB-A 2.0; one USB 2.0 header is exposed internally.

usb-c-ports-data.comment: USB Type-C handles USB 2.0 data, DP 1.4 alt-mode video, and PD power input.

poe-support.comment: No PoE; standard 12 V DC or USB-C PD only.

hdmi-outputs.comment: 1× HDMI 2.0b. Up to three displays total via HDMI + USB-C DP-Alt + internal eDP.

max-display-resolution.comment: 4096×2160 @ 60 Hz on HDMI 2.0b or DP 1.4 (USB-C). Dual 4K@60 across both outputs.

displayport.comment: DP 1.4 via USB Type-C alt-mode (no native DP connector).

mipi-dsi.comment: No MIPI-DSI; an internal eDP 30-pin (2-lane) header is present instead.

mipi-csi.comment: No MIPI-CSI camera connector; cameras connect via USB.

gpio-pins.comment: Two double-row Arduino-style headers expose ~20 digital I/O + 12 analog inputs plus power/UART/I2C/SPI/RS232/audio control pins. Not a standard 40-pin RPi GPIO header; total pin count is approximate.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: Header is a custom LattePanda Leonardo-style layout, not the Raspberry Pi 40-pin pinout.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Different header layout, mounting holes, and EEPROM scheme. RPi HATs do not fit.

pcie-lanes-exposed.value: 1× PCIe 3.0 x2 (M.2 M-key) + 1× PCIe 3.0 x1 (M.2 B-key)
pcie-lanes-exposed.comment: M-key 2280 is x2; B-key (2242/2252/2280) optionally provides x1 PCIe alongside SATA/USB.

i2c-spi-uart.comment: I²C, SPI, UART exposed via the ATmega32U4 Arduino headers and chipset pins.

idle-power-w.comment: ETA Prime measured ~3.7 W idle. Tom's Hardware reported lower (~1.5 W) under more aggressive idle conditions; the higher figure is more typical of a desktop-idle Linux/Windows install.

load-power-w.comment: ~15.9 W ETA Prime / ~18 W Tom's Hardware under stress; vendor TDP is 10 W but real package power exceeds it under sustained all-core load.

cooling-required.comment: Ships with an active fan-and-heatsink assembly; vendor explicitly states a fan is required for sustained turbo.

rtc-battery-header.comment: Onboard CR927 3 V RTC battery + an external RTC power pin.

width-mm.comment: Official docs list 125×78×16 mm. Some third-party listings give 76 mm width; trust vendor docs.

form-factor-class.comment: Pocket-sized x86 SBC; not Pi-standard, Pico-ITX, nor Nano-ITX dimensions.

mounting-hole-pattern.comment: LattePanda-specific mounting pattern; LattePanda offers VESA brackets but no RPi mounting compatibility.

weight-g.comment: Bare-board weight not published in vendor docs and not measured in primary reviews; left unknown.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: Standard x86-64 platform; mainline Linux supports CPU, iGPU (i915), AX201 Wi-Fi (iwlwifi), I225-V (igc), and NVMe out of the box on recent kernels.

official-os-image.comment: Ships with Windows 10 / 11; vendor publishes an Ubuntu 22.04 image and install guide.

distros-with-images.comment: Vendor only documents an Ubuntu image; any generic x86-64 Linux distro will boot, but other board-specific images are not published.

bootloader.comment: Standard Intel/AMI UEFI BIOS with TPM 2.0.

long-term-support.comment: DFRobot/LattePanda has not published a formal support window; standard x86 ecosystem means OS updates remain available even if vendor goes silent.

android-support.comment: No official Android-x86 image from the vendor.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Base price (no Windows license) at DFRobot as of 2026-04-26.

msrp-top-usd.comment: Bundle with Windows 10 Enterprise license; no higher-RAM SKU exists.

available-regions.comment: Sold by DFRobot directly and by resellers in North America (Amazon, Micro Center), Europe (Botland, Kiwi-Electronics), and Asia-Pacific (youyeetoo).

typical-lead-time.comment: In stock at DFRobot and Amazon as of 2026-04-26.

documentation-quality.comment: docs.lattepanda.com has dedicated specification, pinout, OS install, and BIOS pages, plus a community forum. No public schematics or PCB files lower it from a 5.

community-size.comment: Active vendor forum and Discord, but the LattePanda community is much smaller than RPi or Rockchip ecosystems.

active-forum-discord.comment: Vendor-run forum at lattepanda.com/forum with regular activity; LattePanda also runs a Discord.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Vendor sells cases, displays, M.2 NIC add-ins, fans, VESA mounts, and power supplies; smaller than the RPi accessory market but solid for a niche x86 SBC.
